# Contract: Transfer Matcher FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `015-transfer-detection` | **Date**: 2026-07-20
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + all-reader + balance-chain + reconciliation + cross-source-dedup + coverage `engine-ffi`
contracts. The "external interface" is the exported UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are
the consumer-facing form. This slice adds **one function** (`detect_transfers`) and **two new records**
(`TransferInput` input + `TransferPair` output). It **reuses** the existing `Decimal` custom type (base-10 `String`
→ Swift `Decimal`), the `NaiveDate` custom type (ISO-8601 `String`), and the `Direction` enum **unchanged**, and
requires **no `uniffi.toml` change**. Money crosses as an exact string (never a float); the only `f64`/`Double` on
this surface is the confidence `score` (it is not money). Mirrors the `compute_coverage`-wraps-`coverage::…` /
`cross_source_duplicates`-wraps-`dedup::…` precedent exactly, including the name-clash handling.

---

## Exported function

### `detect_transfers`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn detect_transfers(rows: Vec<TransferInput>) -> Vec<TransferPair>` |
| Swift | `public func detectTransfers(rows: [TransferInput]) -> [TransferPair]` |

**Contract**

- Pure single-pool matcher over **one** list of already-parsed, still-unpaired rows for **one user** (the platform
  scopes the list). **Never** reads the network/**clock**/locale/global state, never opens a file/PDF, never queries
  a database (FR-012/014).
- Returns the detected transfer pairs (each `outflow_id` / `inflow_id` / `is_credit_card_payment` / `score`),
  **ordered by the anchor's `(date, id)`**; **empty** when no pair is found (including empty or no-outflow input) —
  never panics (FR-002, SC-008/009).
- **Pairing**: anchors on **outflows** (`Direction::Debit`) in `(date, id)` order; greedily claims the best
  opposite-direction **inflow** (`Direction::Credit`) on a **different account** within **±1 day** and **±₹1.00**
  (both inclusive), resolving ambiguity by `(date_diff, amount_diff, -narration_similarity, id)` (lowest wins); each
  row is claimed **at most once** (FR-003/004/005/006/007).
- `is_credit_card_payment` is **true** when **either** leg is a credit-card account, else **false** (FR-009).
  `score` = `max(0, 1 − 0.2·date_diff − 0.2·amount_diff + 0.2·narration_similarity)` — floored at 0.0, **not** capped
  at 1.0 (FR-010).
- **Purity / totality**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no failure is surfaced to Swift (total function)
  (FR-012, SC-009). **Money is exact `Decimal`**; only `score` is a `Double`, because it is a confidence metric, not
  money (FR-011).

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**

- Build `[TransferInput]` with `amount` as a native Swift `Decimal` (e.g.
  `Decimal(string: "5000.00", locale: Locale(identifier: "en_US_POSIX"))`), `date`/leg fields as shown below, and
  `direction` as `.debit` (outflow) / `.credit` (inflow). Pass the single list; there is **no** two-list variant.
- Read each returned `TransferPair`'s `outflowId` / `inflowId` (the two legs), `isCreditCardPayment` (the Self
  Transfer vs Credit Card Bill Payment split), and `score` (a `Double` confidence metric to rank/threshold —
  higher for closer date/amount + narration overlap). The platform performs all persistence and categorisation
  (this call does none) (FR-014/016).

> Internally: the exported `detect_transfers` wraps `crate::transfer::detect_transfers(&rows)` — passing the owned
> `Vec` as a **borrowed** slice, exactly as `compute_coverage` wraps `coverage::compute_coverage(today,
> &statements, &transactions)` and `cross_source_duplicates` wraps `dedup::cross_source_duplicates(&existing,
> &incoming)`. Because the FFI wrapper name `detect_transfers` **shadows** the pure function, `ffi.rs` imports only
> the transfer **types** (not the pure fn) and calls it fully-qualified. `lib.rs` re-exports the **FFI** function
> (`pub use ffi::detect_transfers;`) and the transfer types (`pub use transfer::{TransferInput, TransferPair};`).
> The pure `transfer::detect_transfers` is **not** re-exported at the crate root (name clash) — `tests/parity.rs`
> uses the FFI-exported one via `kaname_core::detect_transfers`.

---

## Types

### `TransferInput` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record` (input)

```swift
public struct TransferInput {
    public var id: String                // stable, opaque row id (also the final selection tiebreak)
    public var accountId: String         // opaque account id (different-account guard)
    public var isCreditCard: Bool        // this leg is a credit-card account
    public var date: String              // ISO-8601 date, e.g. "2026-06-01"
    public var amount: Decimal           // exact money (base-10 string on the wire) — never a float
    public var direction: Direction      // .debit = outflow (anchor) / .credit = inflow (counterpart)
    public var description: String       // raw narration (un-normalized) for token-Jaccard similarity
}
```

### `TransferPair` (NEW) — `uniffi::Record` (output)

```swift
public struct TransferPair {
    public var outflowId: String            // the anchor (Debit) leg id
    public var inflowId: String             // the counterpart (Credit) leg id
    public var isCreditCardPayment: Bool    // true when either leg is a credit-card account
    public var score: Double                // web _score confidence metric (floored at 0.0, not capped at 1.0)
}
```

Both records derive `Debug/Clone/PartialEq/uniffi::Record` (no serde — the parity harness builds them from typed
fixture rows / a separate `ExpectedPair`). `amount` crosses as a base-10 `String` via the existing `Decimal` custom
type; `date` as an ISO-8601 `String` via the existing `NaiveDate` custom type; `direction` via the existing
`Direction` enum; `score` as a native `Double`. Field/case names are lower-camel-cased by UniFFI.

### `Direction` (REUSED — unchanged) — `uniffi::Enum`

| Rust | Swift |
|-|-|
| `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` | `.debit` (outflow) / `.credit` (inflow) |

### `Decimal` / `NaiveDate` (REUSED — unchanged)

- `TransferInput.amount` crosses as an exact base-10 `String` via the existing
  `uniffi::custom_type!(Decimal, String, …)` and surfaces in Swift as a native `Foundation.Decimal` — **never** a
  float (constitution: money is never a float).
- `TransferInput.date` crosses as an ISO-8601 (`%Y-%m-%d`) `String` via the existing
  `uniffi::custom_type!(NaiveDate, String, …)`. **No new custom type, no `uniffi.toml` change.**

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-020/023)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `transfer.rs` unit tests):

- `transfer_detection_matches_expected` (parity): load `fixtures/transfer/basic.json`, build the `Vec<TransferInput>`
  pool (parse ISO dates + `Decimal::from_str` amounts), call `detect_transfers`, and assert the returned
  `Vec<TransferPair>` equals the `expected_pairs` — the `score` field compared with **exact `f64` `==`** (SC-010).
- `transfer.rs` unit tests additionally cover the five pairing scenarios, the four guards, the boundary
  (1 day / ₹1.00 inclusive), the narration + id tiebreaks, the card flag, the score floor + no-cap,
  greedy single-claim, `narration_similarity` (incl. empty/blank), and empty/no-outflow + determinism
  (SC-001..SC-009).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`, `ios/Tests/TransferDetectionTests.swift`)** — "core ↔ Swift transfer
detection" (mirrors `CoverageTests`/`CrossSourceDedupTests`):

- Build a small `[TransferInput]` — a self-transfer pair (both `isCreditCard: false`) and a credit-card-payment
  pair (one leg `isCreditCard: true`) — with `amount` via `Decimal(string:locale:)` (`en_US_POSIX`), `direction`
  `.debit`/`.credit`. Call `detectTransfers(rows:)`.
- Assert the returned `[TransferPair]`: the self-transfer pair links the two ids with `isCreditCardPayment == false`;
  the card pair has `isCreditCardPayment == true`; read a `score` (a `Double`). Fields surface as
  `outflowId`/`inflowId`/`isCreditCardPayment`/`score`; input as `isCreditCard`. Any comment sits on its **own line
  above** the code (swift-format `[Spacing]` forbids trailing inline `//`).

---

## Stability / compatibility

- Adding one function + two records is additive; no existing signature changes, and `Decimal` / `NaiveDate` /
  `Direction` are reused unchanged, so existing callers, fixtures, and the generated Swift for prior slices are
  unaffected.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts, rebuilt by
  `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**, not the generated files, is
  the reviewed source of truth.
